title: rosa × collina var. flexuosa
intro: Rosa × collina var. flexuosa, commonly known as the wild rose, is a deciduous shrub with fragrant flowers that belong to the rose family (Rosaceae). This plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and antioxidant properties, which are attributed to its high content of flavonoids and phenolic acids. The health benefits of Rosa × collina var. flexuosa include reducing stress, improving sleep, and alleviating symptoms of menopause, as well as providing relief from digestive issues and skin problems. The herbal preparations of this plant include tea, infusions, and tinctures, which can be used to make topical creams and salves for skin care and wound healing.
